name: AWS Certified Security – Specialty (SCS-C03)
description: Specialty certification for security practitioners securing AWS workloads. Validates ability to implement detection, incident response, infrastructure security, IAM, data protection, and governance. 3-5 years experience securing cloud solutions. 65 questions (50 scored + 15 unscored), 170 minutes, 750/1000 passing score.
exportIntroQuestions:
## About this document

This document is a collection of practice exam questions for the **AWS Certified Security – Specialty (SCS-C03)** certification. The questions were selected because they represent realistic security scenarios commonly encountered when securing workloads and architectures on AWS.

### Certification context

This certification validates the ability to design and implement security solutions in the AWS Cloud, including topics such as:

* Designing security monitoring and alerting (GuardDuty, Security Hub, CloudTrail)
* Implementing incident response plans and automated remediation
* Securing infrastructure at the edge, network, and compute layers
* Designing authentication and authorization strategies (IAM, SSO, federation)
* Implementing data protection, encryption, and key management (KMS, CloudHSM)
* Establishing security governance and compliance frameworks

### About the questions

Each question in this document is independent and should be analyzed as a standalone scenario. Practice exams intentionally present isolated situations, and questions may appear in any order during the actual certification exam. Information from one question should never be assumed to apply to another.

For each scenario, this document includes:

* The original practice exam question.
* An explanation of the security reasoning behind the correct answer.
* A discussion of why the remaining options are less appropriate for the stated requirements.

### Learning objective

The primary goal of this material is to develop security-focused reasoning rather than memorization. The focus should be on understanding the threat models and compliance requirements presented in each scenario, identifying the AWS security services and controls that best mitigate the risks, and recognizing the trade-offs between security posture, operational complexity, and cost.

By studying the decision-making process behind each answer, readers can build the specialized security skills expected for the AWS Specialty certification exam.
exportIntroTranscripts:
## About this document

This document contains technical summaries of lesson transcripts related to the **AWS Certified Security – Specialty (SCS-C03)** certification.

### Purpose
Organizes and synthesizes lesson content in a structured format for quick review and deeper understanding of the concepts presented.
exportIntroChat:
## About this document

This document contains AI study conversations related to the **AWS Certified Security – Specialty (SCS-C03)** certification.

### Purpose
Records discussions and explanations generated during interactive AI study sessions, serving as complementary reference material.

domains.name: Detection
domains.description: Domain 1 (16%): Design and implement security monitoring and alerting. Troubleshoot security monitoring. Design logging solutions (CloudTrail, VPC Flow Logs, Config, GuardDuty, Security Hub).

domains.name: Incident Response
domains.description: Domain 2 (14%): Design and implement incident response plans. Detect security threats and anomalies. Respond to compromised resources and credentials.

domains.name: Infrastructure Security
domains.description: Domain 3 (18%): Design security controls for edge services (CloudFront, WAF, Shield). Design network security (VPC, security groups, NACLs, Network Firewall). Troubleshoot network connectivity.

domains.name: Identity and Access Management
domains.description: Domain 4 (20%): Design authentication and authorization (IAM, SSO, federation, Organizations). Design authorization policies. Troubleshoot access issues.

domains.name: Data Protection
domains.description: Domain 5 (18%): Design data encryption strategies (KMS, CloudHSM). Implement key management and rotation. Design data access controls.

domains.name: Security Foundations and Governance
domains.description: Domain 6 (14%): Develop security governance strategy (Organizations, SCPs, Control Tower). Implement compliance automation. Manage security findings and remediation.
